title: passiflora multiflora var. glabra
intro: Passiflora multiflora var. glabra is a variety of passionflower, a flowering vine native to South America, characterized by its smooth, hairless stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its sedative and anti-anxiety properties, as it contains flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Research suggests that Passiflora multiflora var. glabra may also have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality, although more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Passiflora multiflora var. glabra can be found in supplement form, often combined with other calming herbs like valerian root or chamomile, and can be consumed as capsules, teas, or tinctures.
